content_en:
Hormuz wanted to cut the fight short by murdering Khalid treacherously. So, no sooner had the two armies engaged in battle than he challenged Khalid to fight a duel. Hormuz instructed some of his men to remain close to him, so that when he gave a signal they should fall on Khalid and kill him. Khalid at once dismounted and before long his foe was killed.

The death of Hormuz had demoralized the Persians, but nevertheless, they fought hard. The Muslims assailed vehemently, but the chain-linked Persian infantry withstood all attacks. The Muslims redoubled their attacks, and the Persians were forced to fall back. The Persians found their chains to be a death trap, and as they retreated held together in chains they suffered heavy causalities. Before the night set in, the Muslims had won the battle.

Thousands of Persians were killed. and thousands of them were taken captives. The war booty that fell into the hands of the Muslims comprised wagons, armor, stores, costly garments, horses and a good amount of money. Four-fifth of the booty was distributed among the Muslim soldiers and one-fifth was sent to the Caliph at Madeenah. So large was the booty that the share of each cavalryman came to a thousand dirhams. The booty included the 100,000-dirham cap of Hormuz studded with diamonds and pearls. The Caliph offered this cap as a present to Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed. The battle of the Chains at Kazima unchained for the Muslims the gate of Iraq. The so-called uncivilized Arabs had defeated the Persians so proud of their civilization extending over a thousand years.

The Battle of Madhaar:

When Hormuz knew of the march of Khalid and his forces to Kazimah, he sent to the king of Persia, Aradesheer, seeking help and reinforcement. The king dispatched an army from Madaa'in, the capital, under the command of Qaarin Ibn Quryans. The Persian army marched along the left bank of the Tigris. They crossed the Tigris at Madhaar, and there they came to know of the defeat of the Persians at Kazimah. Qaarin camped at Madhaar, and soon the remnants of the army of Hormuz who had escaped from Kazimah joined the camp.

Qaarin was shocked that the imperial army of Persia under such a General as Hormuz should have been defeated by the uncouth Arabs. He resolved that he would avenge the defeat of Kazimah and drive the Arabs to the desert.

In the meantime, the advance guard of Al-Muthanna, who scoured the countryside, came to know of the Persian concentration at Madhaar. Al-Muthanna sent news of the situat..n to Khalid and halted opposite the Persians at Madhaar. O the other hand, Qaarin got information that some Muslim forces were lurking in the neighborhood, and his plan was to fall on this Muslim force and destroy it, before it could get help from the main Muslim army.

Khalid realized the danger that beset the column of AlMuthanna. Khalid was keen that he should destroy the Persian force under Qaarin while the impact of the defeat at Kazimah was still fresh in the Persian mind. By forced marches, Khalid reached Madhaar in the month of Safar, 12 AH, before Qaarin could take any action against the column of Al-Muthanna. The Persian forces fought with great tenacity, but the Muslims finally achieved victory. Qaarin and almost 30,000 soldiers of the Persian army were killed in the battle. It was also the second victory the Muslims won against the Persians. Heavy spoils were won in the battle, which exceeded the booty gained at Kazimah. Four-fifths of the spoils were distributed among the soldiers and one-fifth share was sent to Madeenah.

The Battle of Walajahh:

Being weakened by two successive defeats, the Persian king, Ardsheer, ordered the assembling of two more Persian armies to fight the Muslims, One army was placed under the

command of Andarzaghar, a military Governor of considerable standing who had grown up among the Arabs, and was familiar with the Arab way of fighting. He commanded considerable popularity among the Arab tribes allied with the Persians. In addition to the regular Persian army, Andarzaghar was commissioned to raise contingents from the Arab auxiliaries. The other force was placed under the direct command of Bahman, the Commander-in-Chief of the Persian forces.

Andarzaghar was required to move with his army to grapple with the Muslims. The other force under Bahman was to follow after some time. The strength of the army of Andarzarghar was very considerable, and if the army of Bahman reinforced it, the Persian army would likely to assume formidable dimensions.

Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, may Allah be pleased with him, weighed up the situation and worked out his strategy. He left two battalions in ambush, so as to attack the enemy from the rear at the right moment. Khalid's strategy, therefore, was that he should tackle the army of Andarzaghar before the main army under Bahman could join it.

By forced marches, Khalid reached Walajah. As Andarzaghar surveyed the field, the Muslim army did not consist of more than 10,000 persons, and the Muslim cavalry was nowhere to be seen. The strength of the Persian army, on the contrary, was thrice the strength of the Muslim army, and Andarzaghar thought that in no time he would be able to make mince meat of the Muslim force and thus avenge the defeats of Kazimah and Madhaar.

The two armies met with a clash of steel, and the battle raged with unabated fury. The Muslims struck at the heavily armed Persians, but the Persians stood their ground, and

repulsed all attacks. Then Andarzaghar ordered a counter attack. The Muslims were able to hold the attack for some time, but as the Persians intensified their pressure, the Muslims began to lose ground and fell back. Andarzaghar exhorted his men to step up their pressure for victory was very much in sight.

At that critical juncture, Khalid gave a signal. The next moment over the crest of the ridge that stretched behind the Persian army appeared columns of mounted Muslim warriors. Raising shouts of "Allahu Akbar" (Allah is Greatest), the Muslim cavalry charged at a gallop, and the plain of Walajahh shook under the thundering hooves of the Arab horse. The Persians who were pressing forward were now caught in a trap. When they turned their face to meet the charge of the Muslim cavalry, the main Muslim army delivered a furious charge. The ring of steel became tighter round the Persians, and in whatever direction they turned, sword and dagger struck them down. Andarzaghar fled from. the battlefield and penetrated deep into the desert where he lost his way and died of thirst.

The battle of Walajah ended in a victory for the Muslims. That was the third consecutive victory of the Muslims over the Persians. The victory of Walajah established the superiority of the Muslim fighting forces. Once again a large booty fell into the hands of the Muslims. Four-fifth of the spoils were distributed among the Muslim warriors on the spot, and the remaining one-fifth were sent to Madeenah. Khalid's remark was: "Behold the incredible heap of spoils! We have attained two goals! Victory in Allah's Cause, and amazing wealth,"

The Battle of Ulays: After their defeats in the battles of Kazimah, Madhaar

and Walajah, the Persians felt that it was necessary to change 104

their strategy. They decided to settle Christian Arabs in preference to Muslim ones. In pursuance of this policy, after the battle of Walajah the Christian Arabs mustered at Ulays, which was 10 miles from Walajah in another bid to drive the Muslims from Iraq. Ardasheer gave orders to Bahmaan to lend them full support. Also, the Persian commander-in-chief decided to send another Persian force to Ulays to reinforce the Christian Arabs. This force was commanded by Jabaan.

When the Muslims heard of the assembling of Christian Arabs and Persians at Ulays, Khalid mustered his forces to encounter them. The Muslims launched an attack against the Persians, but the latter stood as a rock and showed no signs of weakness. The Muslims renewed the charge, but expecting help from Bahmaan the Persians proved stubborn and persistent and offered stiff resistance. The Muslim attack did not yield the desired result, and as they appeared to lose force, a counter attack from the Persians was expected. In view of the limited space, there were no possibilities of a maneuver here, and Khalid was afraid that in a frontal attack, the Persians in view of their superiority of strength had the advantage and were likely to carry the day.

At this critical situation, Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, may Allah be pleased with him, resorted to Almighty Allah and prayed for victory: "O Allah, if you grant us victory, I shall see that no enemy warrior is left alive until the river runs red with their blood." It was a typical soldier's pledge, and it inspired Khalid and his men to greater sacrifice and fighting. The Persian resistance ultimately broke down. By the afternoon a greater part of the Persian and Christian Arab army had been shattered, and the battle was over. The Muslims had secured a brilliant victory against the Persians for the fourth time.

Of all the battles fought by Khalid, the battle of Ulays was the toughest and the fiercest. According to At-Tabari, 70,000 Persian and Christian Arabs lost their lives as a result of the battle of Ulays. The Ulays disaster unnerved the Persian and the Christian Arabs. The local inhabitants of the region of Ulays entered into a pact with the Muslims. They agreed to pay the Jizyan in lieu of Muslim protection. They also undertook to act as in ormers and guides for the Muslims.

The Conquest of Al-Heerah:

After the battle of Ulays, the road to Al-Heerah lay open. Khalid and his army immediately took the road to Al-Heerah.

This city had been the capital of the Iraqi Arabs since the second century of the Christian era. After the battle of Ulays all the rebels took refuge there. Its Persian governor, AlMarzabaan (Azarbeh), came to know of the advance of the Muslim force and so he set about defending the city. He sent forward a cavalry group commanded by his son to hold the advance of the Muslims. This cavalry group was commissioned to dam the Euphrates in order to hold the advance of the Muslim army.

In the advance to Al-Heerah, while the main Muslim army marched by riding on camels and horses, the heavy military loads were carried by boat on the river. The Muslim forces had traversed a short distance only, when due to the damming of the river, the water level fell, and the boats carrying the military loads were grounded. Seeing this situation, Khalid dashed off at great speed on the road to Al-Heerah at the head of a cavalry detachment. At Badgalah, some twelve miles from Al-Heerah, the son of Azarbeh and his column were surprised, and were cut down to one man. Khalid thereafter
content_id:
Hormuz ingin mengakhiri pertempuran dengan cepat melalui pembunuhan Khalid secara licik. Maka, begitu kedua pasukan terlibat dalam pertempuran, ia menantang Khalid untuk bertarung satu lawan satu. Hormuz memerintahkan beberapa pasukannya untuk tetap berada di dekatnya, sehingga ketika ia memberi sinyal, mereka akan menyerang Khalid dan membunuhnya. Khalid langsung turun dari kudanya dan tidak lama kemudian musuhnya pun terbunuh.

Kematian Hormuz telah melemahkan semangat pasukan Persia, namun demikian, mereka tetap bertempur dengan gigih. Kaum Muslimin menyerang dengan sengit, namun infanteri Persia yang dirantai satu sama lain mampu menahan semua serangan. Kaum Muslimin melipatgandakan serangan mereka, dan pasukan Persia terpaksa mundur. Pasukan Persia mendapati bahwa rantai-rantai mereka menjadi perangkap maut, dan saat mereka mundur dalam keadaan saling terikat rantai, mereka menderita banyak korban jiwa. Sebelum malam tiba, kaum Muslimin telah memenangkan pertempuran.

Ribuan pasukan Persia terbunuh dan ribuan lainnya ditawan. Harta rampasan perang yang jatuh ke tangan kaum Muslimin meliputi gerobak, baju baju besi, perbekalan, pakaian mewah, kuda, dan sejumlah besar uang. Empat perlima dari harta rampasan tersebut dibagikan kepada para prajurit Muslim dan seperlima dikirimkan kepada Khalifah di Madinah. Harta rampasan itu begitu besar sehingga bagian setiap penunggang kuda mencapai seribu dirham. Harta rampasan tersebut termasuk topi Hormuz senilai 100.000 dirham yang dihiasi berlian dan mutiara. Khalifah menghadiahkan topi ini kepada Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed. Pertempuran Rantai di Kazima telah membuka pintu Iraq bagi kaum Muslimin. Bangsa Arab yang dianggap tidak berperadaban telah mengalahkan bangsa Persia yang begitu bangga dengan peradaban mereka yang membentang lebih dari seribu tahun.

Pertempuran Madhaar:

Ketika Hormuz mengetahui pergerakan Khalid dan pasukannya menuju Kazimah, ia mengirim pesan kepada raja Persia, Ardasheer, untuk meminta bantuan dan bala bantuan. Raja mengutus sebuah pasukan dari Mada'in, ibu kota kerajaan, di bawah komando Qaarin Ibn Quryans. Pasukan Persia bergerak di sepanjang tepi kiri Sungai Tigris. Mereka menyeberangi Sungai Tigris di Madhaar, dan di sanalah mereka mengetahui kekalahan pasukan Persia di Kazimah. Qaarin berkemah di Madhaar, dan tak lama kemudian sisa-sisa pasukan Hormuz yang berhasil melarikan diri dari Kazimah bergabung dengan perkemahan tersebut.

Qaarin sangat terkejut bahwa pasukan kekaisaran Persia di bawah seorang panglima sekaliber Hormuz bisa dikalahkan oleh bangsa Arab yang dianggap kasar. Ia bertekad untuk membalas kekalahan di Kazimah dan mengusir bangsa Arab kembali ke padang pasir.

Sementara itu, pasukan garda depan Al-Muthanna yang menjelajahi pedesaan mengetahui adanya konsentrasi pasukan Persia di Madhaar. Al-Muthanna mengirimkan berita tentang situasi tersebut kepada Khalid dan berhenti berhadapan dengan pasukan Persia di Madhaar. Di pihak lain, Qaarin mendapat informasi bahwa sejumlah pasukan Muslim sedang mengintai di sekitar wilayah itu, dan rencananya adalah menyerang dan menghancurkan pasukan Muslim tersebut sebelum mendapat bantuan dari pasukan utama kaum Muslimin.

Khalid menyadari bahaya yang mengancam kolom pasukan Al-Muthanna. Khalid sangat ingin menghancurkan pasukan Persia di bawah komando Qaarin sementara dampak kekalahan di Kazimah masih terasa segar dalam benak pasukan Persia. Dengan pawai paksa, Khalid tiba di Madhaar pada bulan Safar, 12 H, sebelum Qaarin sempat mengambil tindakan apa pun terhadap kolom pasukan Al-Muthanna. Pasukan Persia bertempur dengan ketangguhan yang luar biasa, namun kaum Muslimin akhirnya meraih kemenangan. Qaarin dan hampir 30.000 prajurit pasukan Persia terbunuh dalam pertempuran tersebut. Inilah pula kemenangan kedua yang diraih kaum Muslimin atas bangsa Persia. Harta rampasan yang sangat besar berhasil diperoleh dalam pertempuran ini, bahkan melebihi harta rampasan yang didapat di Kazimah. Empat perlima dari harta rampasan dibagikan kepada para prajurit dan seperlima dikirimkan ke Madinah.

Pertempuran Walajah:

Karena melemah akibat dua kekalahan berturut-turut, raja Persia Ardasheer memerintahkan penghimpunan dua pasukan Persia lagi untuk memerangi kaum Muslimin. Satu pasukan ditempatkan di bawah

komando Andarzaghar, seorang Gubernur militer yang cukup berpengaruh dan telah tumbuh besar di kalangan bangsa Arab serta memahami cara berperang mereka. Ia cukup populer di kalangan suku-suku Arab yang bersekutu dengan Persia. Selain pasukan Persia reguler, Andarzaghar juga ditugaskan untuk menghimpun kontingen dari pasukan tambahan Arab. Pasukan lainnya ditempatkan langsung di bawah komando Bahman, Panglima Tertinggi pasukan Persia.

Andarzaghar diperintahkan untuk bergerak bersama pasukannya guna menghadapi kaum Muslimin. Pasukan lainnya di bawah Bahman akan menyusul beberapa waktu kemudian. Kekuatan pasukan Andarzaghar sangat besar, dan jika pasukan Bahman bergabung dengannya, pasukan Persia kemungkinan besar akan mencapai kekuatan yang sangat tangguh.

Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, radhiyallahu 'anhu, menimbang situasi tersebut dan menyusun strateginya. Ia menempatkan dua batalion dalam penyergapan untuk menyerang musuh dari belakang pada saat yang tepat. Strategi Khalid adalah menghadapi pasukan Andarzaghar sebelum pasukan utama di bawah Bahman sempat bergabung dengannya.

Dengan pawai paksa, Khalid tiba di Walajah. Ketika Andarzaghar mengamati medan, pasukan Muslim tidak lebih dari 10.000 orang, dan pasukan berkuda Muslim tidak tampak sama sekali. Kekuatan pasukan Persia, sebaliknya, tiga kali lipat kekuatan pasukan Muslim, dan Andarzaghar berpikir bahwa dalam waktu singkat ia akan mampu menghancurkan pasukan Muslim dan membalas kekalahan di Kazimah dan Madhaar.

Kedua pasukan bertemu dalam bentrokan senjata, dan pertempuran pun berkecamuk dengan dahsyat tanpa mereda. Kaum Muslimin menyerang pasukan Persia yang bersenjata berat, namun pasukan Persia bertahan dan

menggagalkan semua serangan. Kemudian Andarzaghar memerintahkan serangan balasan. Kaum Muslimin mampu menahan serangan tersebut untuk beberapa waktu, namun saat pasukan Persia meningkatkan tekanan, kaum Muslimin mulai kehilangan posisi dan mundur. Andarzaghar menyemangati pasukannya untuk meningkatkan tekanan karena kemenangan sudah tampak di depan mata.

Pada saat kritis itulah, Khalid memberi sinyal. Seketika itu juga, di atas puncak punggung bukit yang membentang di belakang pasukan Persia, muncullah barisan prajurit Muslim berkuda. Dengan seruan "Allahu Akbar" (Allah Maha Besar), pasukan berkuda Muslim menyerbu dengan kecepatan penuh, dan dataran Walajah berguncang di bawah derap kaki kuda Arab yang menggelegar. Pasukan Persia yang sedang maju kini terjebak dalam perangkap. Ketika mereka berbalik untuk menghadapi serangan pasukan berkuda Muslim, pasukan utama kaum Muslimin pun melancarkan serangan dahsyat. Kepungan baja semakin rapat mengurung pasukan Persia, dan ke mana pun mereka berpaling, pedang dan belati menghantam mereka. Andarzaghar melarikan diri dari medan perang dan masuk jauh ke dalam padang pasir di mana ia tersesat dan mati kehausan.

Pertempuran Walajah berakhir dengan kemenangan bagi kaum Muslimin. Itulah kemenangan ketiga berturut-turut kaum Muslimin atas bangsa Persia. Kemenangan Walajah menegaskan keunggulan kekuatan tempur kaum Muslimin. Sekali lagi harta rampasan yang besar jatuh ke tangan kaum Muslimin. Empat perlima dari harta rampasan dibagikan di tempat kepada para prajurit Muslim, dan seperlima sisanya dikirimkan ke Madinah. Komentar Khalid adalah: "Lihatlah tumpukan harta rampasan yang luar biasa ini! Kami telah mencapai dua tujuan! Kemenangan di jalan Allah, dan kekayaan yang menakjubkan."

Pertempuran Ulays: Setelah kekalahan dalam pertempuran Kazimah, Madhaar

dan Walajah, bangsa Persia merasa perlu untuk mengubah strategi mereka. Mereka memutuskan untuk mengutamakan bangsa Arab Kristen daripada bangsa Arab Muslim. Dalam rangka melaksanakan kebijakan ini, setelah pertempuran Walajah bangsa Arab Kristen berkumpul di Ulays, yang berjarak 10 mil dari Walajah, dalam upaya lain untuk mengusir kaum Muslimin dari Iraq. Ardasheer memerintahkan Bahmaan untuk memberikan dukungan penuh kepada mereka. Selain itu, panglima tertinggi Persia memutuskan untuk mengirimkan pasukan Persia lainnya ke Ulays guna memperkuat bangsa Arab Kristen. Pasukan ini dipimpin oleh Jabaan.

Ketika kaum Muslimin mendengar penghimpunan bangsa Arab Kristen dan pasukan Persia di Ulays, Khalid pun mengumpulkan pasukannya untuk menghadapi mereka. Kaum Muslimin melancarkan serangan terhadap pasukan Persia, namun pasukan Persia berdiri kokoh bagaikan batu karang dan tidak menunjukkan tanda-tanda kelemahan. Kaum Muslimin memperbarui serangan, namun karena mengharapkan bantuan dari Bahmaan, pasukan Persia tetap keras kepala dan gigih serta memberikan perlawanan yang sengit. Serangan kaum Muslimin tidak membuahkan hasil yang diinginkan, dan saat serangan tampak mulai melemah, serangan balasan dari pasukan Persia pun diperkirakan akan segera terjadi. Mengingat ruang gerak yang terbatas, tidak ada kemungkinan untuk melakukan manuver di sana, dan Khalid khawatir bahwa dalam serangan frontal, pasukan Persia dengan keunggulan kekuatan yang dimiliki akan lebih diuntungkan dan kemungkinan besar akan memenangkan pertempuran.

Dalam situasi kritis ini, Khalid Ibn Al-Waleed, radhiyallahu 'anhu, bersandar kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa dan berdoa memohon kemenangan: "Ya Allah, jika Engkau menganugerahkan kemenangan kepada kami, aku akan memastikan bahwa tidak ada satu pun prajurit musuh yang tersisa hidup hingga sungai mengalir merah dengan darah mereka." Itulah sumpah khas seorang prajurit, dan hal itu menginspirasi Khalid dan pasukannya untuk berkorban lebih besar dan bertempur lebih gigih. Perlawanan Persia akhirnya runtuh. Menjelang sore, sebagian besar pasukan Persia dan Arab Kristen telah dihancurkan, dan pertempuran pun berakhir. Kaum Muslimin telah meraih kemenangan yang gemilang atas bangsa Persia untuk keempat kalinya.

Dari semua pertempuran yang dilancarkan oleh Khalid, pertempuran Ulays adalah yang paling berat dan paling sengit. Menurut At-Tabari, 70.000 orang Persia dan Arab Kristen tewas akibat pertempuran Ulays. Malapetaka Ulays membuat gentar orang-orang Persia dan Arab Kristen. Penduduk setempat di wilayah Ulays mengadakan perjanjian dengan kaum Muslimin. Mereka sepakat membayar Jizyah sebagai imbalan atas perlindungan Muslim. Mereka juga berjanji untuk bertindak sebagai informan dan pemandu bagi kaum Muslimin.

Penaklukan Al-Heerah:

Setelah pertempuran Ulays, jalan menuju Al-Heerah terbuka lebar. Khalid dan pasukannya segera mengambil jalan menuju Al-Heerah.

Kota ini telah menjadi ibu kota bangsa Arab Irak sejak abad kedua era Masehi. Setelah pertempuran Ulays, semua pemberontak berlindung di sana. Gubernur Persianya, Al-Marzabaan (Azarbeh), mengetahui kemajuan pasukan Muslim sehingga ia mulai mempertahankan kota tersebut. Ia mengirimkan sekelompok pasukan berkuda yang dipimpin oleh putranya untuk menahan kemajuan kaum Muslimin. Pasukan berkuda ini ditugaskan untuk membendung Sungai Eufrat guna menghambat laju pasukan Muslim.

Dalam perjalanan menuju Al-Heerah, sementara pasukan Muslim utama berbaris dengan menunggang unta dan kuda, beban militer yang berat diangkut dengan perahu di atas sungai. Pasukan Muslim baru menempuh jarak yang singkat, ketika akibat pembendungan sungai, permukaan air menurun, dan perahu-perahu yang membawa muatan militer pun terdampar. Melihat situasi ini, Khalid bergegas dengan kecepatan tinggi di jalan menuju Al-Heerah sambil memimpin satuan pasukan berkuda. Di Badgalah, sekitar dua belas mil dari Al-Heerah, putra Azarbeh beserta pasukannya dikejutkan dan dihabisi hingga hanya tersisa satu orang. Setelah itu Khalid
